attacking_style: Pure sprinter who relies on team-delivered positioning rather than late attacks. Performs best on slight uphill ramps where power-to-weight gives marginal advantage over pure flat sprinters.
team_tactics: Soudal-Quick-Step deploys long, energy-intensive leadout trains that can be beaten by shorter, better-timed efforts. Team willing to opportunistically position him even during GC-focused stages when peloton fractures.
terrain_preference: Slight ramp finishes where climbing ability separates pure sprinters (Catalunya Stage 3 victory). Struggles on long drags and selective finishes that require sustained climbing power (Romandie).
vulnerability: Dropped on any sustained climbing or long drags. Team's long leadout trains waste energy in technical finales, leaving him exposed to better-timed rival efforts. Lacks firepower against top-tier sprinters on pure flat finishes.
key_patterns: Dropped on selective finishes with sustained climbing; cannot contest beyond flat/slight ramp terrain.
Team deploys long leadout trains that get outmaneuvered by shorter, energy-conserving rival efforts.
Best results on slight uphill finishes where power beats pure speed sprinters.
Quick-Step opportunistically positions him even during GC-focused races when peloton control weakens.
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 Dauphiné showed insufficient firepower versus top sprinters despite heavy team investment, downward trend from 2022 Catalunya success
